title: Will Bitcoin reach $76,000 April 13-19?
description: What price will Bitcoin hit April 13-19?

case.title: Will Bitcoin reach $76,000 April 13-19?
case.description: What price will Bitcoin hit April 13-19?

initial_forecast.question: Will Bitcoin reach $76,000 April 13-19?
initial_forecast.time_horizon_label: Apr 13-19 intrawindow Binance high mechanics through week-end resolution
initial_forecast.rationale_summary: BTC reaching $76k this week remains more likely than not because only one Binance 1-minute high is needed and spot is already close, but the market's 75% price still looks too rich relative to bounded evidence on momentum persistence, so the disciplined posture is watch-only rather than chasing Yes.

latest_forecast.question: Will Bitcoin reach $76,000 April 13-19?
latest_forecast.rationale_summary: Bitcoin very likely already satisfied this contract because bundled Binance data showed an in-window high above $76,000, but since the market is already at 0.9995 and the only residual risk is narrow settlement-surface parity or administrative correction risk, the disciplined output is watch-only rather than fresh action.

forecast_rows.question: Will Bitcoin reach $76,000 April 13-19?
forecast_rows.rationale_summary: Bitcoin very likely already satisfied this contract because bundled Binance data showed an in-window high above $76,000, but since the market is already at 0.9995 and the only residual risk is narrow settlement-surface parity or administrative correction risk, the disciplined output is watch-only rather than fresh action.

key_assumptions: Touch-based contracts resolve more often than equivalent close-based contracts.
Recent BTC volatility remains sufficient for a brief wick through 76000.
No sharp risk-off regime break pushes BTC materially away from the threshold.
strongest_supports: Polymarket rules settle on any Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute high at or above 76000.
Recent Binance and Coinbase data put BTC highs around 75.4k-75.5k, only about 1% below target.
Nearly six days remained in the window when checked.
strongest_disconfirmers: BTC had not yet clearly touched 76000 in the current window when checked despite already trading near it.
Near-threshold setups can fail through repeated rejection and stall.
persona_runs.timing_relevance: Short-dated weekly market with nearly six days remaining from the time checked; remaining time is a major part of the Yes case.
persona_runs.source_quality_view: High quality for rules/pricing from Polymarket and strong recent price context from Binance plus Coinbase, with medium evidence independence and low source-of-truth ambiguity after verification.

key_assumptions: With BTC already within roughly 1% of the threshold, realized volatility matters more than any single scheduled catalyst.
The contract benchmark is reasonably aligned with broad BTC spot behavior.
No major negative shock interrupts momentum before April 19.
strongest_supports: CoinGecko showed BTC around $75,409, only about $591 below the threshold.
The 2-day hourly path showed BTC rallying from roughly $70.9k to the mid-$75k area.
Several trading days remained in the contract window.
strongest_disconfirmers: The market may be over-crediting proximity alone while underweighting mean-reversion risk.
The exact Polymarket benchmark/rules text was not fully visible in the fetch, so a broad-market print may not perfectly map to settlement.
persona_runs.timing_relevance: This is a short-dated threshold market where proximity to the barrier and remaining calendar time are themselves the main catalysts.
persona_runs.source_quality_view: Adequate for a low-difficulty case: primary contract surface plus direct live-price data, but only medium independence and medium rule ambiguity because the full rules text was not captured.

key_assumptions: BTC only needs an ordinary short-horizon volatility burst from current spot to touch $76k once during the window.
The designated settlement source will behave close enough to broad spot pricing that a true market-wide $76k tag would count.
The contract is effectively about a threshold touch/high, not maintaining the level.
strongest_supports: Binance, Kraken, and Coinbase all showed BTC around $75.3k-$75.4k, less than 1% below the threshold.
CoinGecko range data showed BTC already moved from roughly $70.7k to $74.8k in the sampled window, indicating active realized volatility.
A multi-day touch contract has a lower hurdle than a close-above contract.
strongest_disconfirmers: No verified source in this run showed an actual $76k print yet.
Polymarket rule-text retrieval was incomplete, leaving medium-high source-of-truth ambiguity for the precise benchmark.
persona_runs.timing_relevance: High; the contract is a multi-day threshold-touch question and remaining time plus near-threshold spot level drive most of the probability.
persona_runs.source_quality_view: Good contextual price evidence from direct spot venues plus CoinGecko, but only partial visibility into the governing Polymarket rule surface.

key_assumptions: Major liquid spot venues are directionally informative proxies for the settlement source.
Cross-venue dispersion near 76k will not be large enough to change the answer materially.
The remaining week offers enough volatility for a roughly 0.8% upside excursion.
strongest_supports: Binance direct data showed highs of 74,900 on Apr 13 UTC and about 75,430 on Apr 14 UTC, leaving only a small gap to 76k.
CoinGecko independently corroborated BTC trading around $75.4k, supporting near-threshold conditions.
Several days remained in the contract window after the checked data.
strongest_disconfirmers: Checked evidence did not yet show an actual 76,000 print.
Polymarket rule/source-of-truth details were not fully legible in accessible fetched text.
A sub-1% reversal from checked levels would be enough to miss.
persona_runs.timing_relevance: High; this is a date-specific threshold-touch market where remaining week volatility and intraperiod path matter more than end-of-week close.
persona_runs.source_quality_view: Adequate for a low-difficulty case: one direct major-exchange source plus one independent aggregator cross-check, but only medium confidence on exact settlement mechanics.

key_assumptions: A one-touch Binance 1-minute-high market should price more generously than a close-above market.
Routine BTC volatility from a mid-$75k starting point is enough to threaten $76k within the remaining window.
Recent strength does not guarantee immediate continuation and can still stall just below the threshold.
strongest_supports: Polymarket rules only require any Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute high at or above $76,000 during Apr 13-19.
Observed Binance 24h high near $75,397 left only about a $603 gap to target.
BTC was already up about 5.6% over 24 hours, showing enough realized range for another sub-1% extension.
strongest_disconfirmers: The actual qualifying Binance high had not happened yet despite the strong move.
Near-threshold markets can fail if momentum exhausts or repeatedly rejects below round-number resistance.
persona_runs.timing_relevance: High; this is a date-specific one-touch threshold contract and short-term price path dominates.
persona_runs.source_quality_view: Good for a low-difficulty case: Polymarket rules established settlement mechanics, Binance provided direct relevant price data, and Coinbase/CoinGecko served as contextual cross-checks.

decision_summary.primary_crux: BTC reaching $76,000 this week is still more likely than not because the contract is a Binance 1-minute high touch market and BTC is already trading within roughly 1% of the threshold, but the market's 75% price remains somewhat too rich without a verified qualifying $76,000 Binance print yet.

causal_attribution.note: Evaluator distinguishes observed market/action facts from inferred causal attributions. Attribution population is intentionally left sparse until structured event + market-path reconstruction is implemented.
